summary: Completed frontend testing of Spectra mode (Urban Energy Stress Mapping) features. All 12 test scenarios passed. ESI heatmap polygons render on map, SpectraSidebar shows correct stats, NeighborhoodDrawer opens/closes correctly, neighborhood selection highlights work, and switching back to geo-energy mode restores original UI.

issues: First 3 rows in the drawer appear to have neighborhood names cut off/not visible at the far left — text may be truncated. Row names like 'Shubra' visible lower down but top rows show only badges.

critical_code_review_comments: ESI neighborhood data is MOCKED (MOCK_SPECTRA_DATA in lib/mockSpectraData.ts) — clearly labeled in sidebar as 'Mock data pre-loaded'
Data Sources section has upload stubs that do nothing on click (stub behavior is acceptable for MVP)
AI Report 'Generate Report' button is a stub with no backend integration
context_for_next_testing_agent: Spectra mode fully functional. All ESI stats correct (8 neighborhoods, 3 high stress, avg ESI 66, 1 critical). ESI polygons render on map in Cairo. Drawer toggle works, 8 rows sortable. Row click selects neighborhood and shows 'Selected Zone' detail in sidebar. Mode switching between Spectra/Geo-Energy fully works. Previous geo-energy backend tests at /app/backend/tests/test_geo_energy.py still valid.
mocked_apis: ESI neighborhood data - all mock Cairo neighborhoods from lib/mockSpectraData.ts
